results.caption:
This email looked perfect. The logo was right. The name was right. The urgency was right. That's the point.

Spear phishing isn't spray-and-pray. It's a surgical strike built from your LinkedIn profile, your company org chart, and your last public conference talk.

The attacker spent 3 hours on you. You had 82 seconds to spot it.

Break down how anatomy of a spear phish gets engineered — and why your brain is the vulnerability being exploited.

#CyberSecurity #Phishing #SocialEngineering #InfoSec #ThreatIntelligence
results.hook: Dissecting a real phishing email like a crime scene autopsy triggers visceral recognition — every security professional has seen this exact email.

results.topic: Pretexting — The Story They Told You
results.caption:
They didn't hack your firewall. They hacked your empathy.

Pretexting is when an attacker builds a complete fictional identity — a fake vendor, a fake IT technician, a fake new colleague — and uses that story to extract information you'd never hand to a stranger.

They study you for days. They know your boss's name, your company's ticketing system, and what your IT team's email signature looks like.

Then they call. And you help them, because that's what helpful people do.

This is MITRE T1264. This is how 43% of social engineering attacks begin.

#Phishing #SocialEngineering #Pretexting #CyberAwareness #SecurityCulture
results.hook: Showing the attacker as a costume-wearing actor building a false identity transforms an abstract concept into a chilling, shareable human story.

results.caption:
Your brain has a vulnerability. It's called urgency.

When cortisol spikes — when you see 'URGENT,' 'FINAL WARNING,' 'YOUR ACCOUNT WILL BE SUSPENDED IN 15 MINUTES' — your prefrontal cortex goes offline. Pattern recognition drops. Critical thinking slows.

Attackers know this. They engineer it deliberately.

Urgency + Authority + Scarcity = Click.

This is the psychological formula behind 78% of successful phishing attacks. You're not gullible. You're human. And that's exactly the exploit.

#Phishing #CyberPsychology #SocialEngineering #HumanRisk #CyberSecurity
results.hook: Visualizing the psychological countdown clock of urgency as a literal bomb defusal scenario makes the neuroscience of phishing viscerally tangible.

results.caption:
You typed your password. Congratulations — so did the attacker.

A credential harvest page can be a 1:1 clone of Microsoft, Google, Okta, or your corporate VPN. Built in under 4 minutes using open-source phishing kits like EvilGinx2 or Modlishka.

The URL looked close. The logo was perfect. The SSL padlock was green.

And in 82 seconds, your credentials were in a Telegram channel in Eastern Europe.

Here's what the forensics look like on the other side of that fake login.

#Phishing #CredentialTheft #CyberSecurity #ZeroTrust #IdentityProtection
results.hook: Showing a pixel-perfect fake login page alongside the real one — with forensic markers revealing the differences — makes every viewer immediately check what they're logging into.

results.caption:
The wire transfer was $2.3M. The CEO 'sent' the email. Except the CEO never sent anything.

Business Email Compromise (BEC) is the #1 most financially damaging cybercrime — $2.9 BILLION lost in 2023 alone (FBI IC3).

No malware. No ransomware. Just an email from 'your CEO' asking finance to process an urgent wire to a new vendor. And a finance manager who trusted it, because the email looked exactly right.

The FBI calls it the '29 Billion Dollar Scam.' Your CFO is the target. Your finance team is the mechanism. Your email system is the weapon.

This is MITRE T1566 + T1534. This is BEC.

#BEC #CEOFraud #BusinessEmailCompromise #CyberSecurity #FinancialCrime
results.hook: Framing CEO fraud as a puppeteer controlling the entire corporate hierarchy from the shadows creates an unforgettable metaphor that CFOs and executives will share immediately.

results.caption:
A threat actor never touched a keyboard. They just called.

Vishing — voice phishing — is social engineering at its most intimate. A caller pretends to be IT support, your bank, or a government agency. They build trust in under 60 seconds. Then they ask for one small thing.

Your credentials. Your OTP. Your access.

And the worst part? It feels completely normal.

🔴 MITRE ATT&CK T1566.004 — Phishing via Voice
🔴 Average call duration before credential handoff: 4 minutes 12 seconds
🔴 67% of vishing victims report the caller 'seemed completely legitimate'

Train your people. Verify out-of-band. Never give credentials over a phone call — ever.

#CyberSecurity #Vishing #SocialEngineering #PhishingAwareness #ZeroTrust
results.hook: The moment a human voice becomes a hacker's most lethal exploit — viewers feel the trap closing in real time.

results.topic: Smishing — The Text That Seemed Real
results.caption:
It arrived between meetings. Between breaths.

A text message. Official-looking. Urgent. Familiar.

'Your account has been locked. Verify now or lose access.'

One tap. That's all it took.

Smishing — SMS phishing — bypasses every email filter you've ever deployed. It lands directly in the most trusted channel humans have: their personal phone.

🔴 MITRE ATT&CK T1660 — Phishing via SMS
🔴 98% of SMS messages are opened. 90% within 3 minutes.
🔴 Fake banking texts up 328% in 2023–2024

If a text asks you to click, call, or confirm — verify through a separate, known channel first.

No exceptions.

#Smishing #MobileSecurity #CyberAwareness #PhishingAttack #InfoSec
results.hook: A glowing phone screen in a dark moment — viewers instantly recognize their own vulnerability in the scene.

results.caption:
Push. Push. Push. Push. Push.

At 2:47 AM, groggy and half-asleep, they tapped 'Approve' just to make it stop.

That's MFA Fatigue — also called MFA Bombing or Prompt Bombing. Attackers flood your authentication app with push notifications until exhaustion, confusion, or frustration causes you to approve one.

No malware. No exploit code. Just relentless psychological pressure.

🔴 MITRE ATT&CK T1621 — Multi-Factor Authentication Request Generation
🔴 Used in the Uber breach (2022), Cisco breach (2022), Microsoft breach (2023)
🔴 Average victim approves the fraudulent request within 12 push attempts

The fix? Number matching. Additional context. FIDO2 passkeys. Hardware tokens.

Fatigue is a feature of the attack — not a bug in your user.

#MFAFatigue #MFABombing #ZeroTrust #CyberSecurity #IdentitySecurity
results.hook: A screen erupting with approval requests captures the exact psychological overwhelm that attackers deliberately engineer.

results.caption:
30 seconds. That's all you need — if you know what to look for.

Every phishing email leaves fingerprints. Most people just aren't trained to see them.

Here's your 30-second checklist:

🔍 1. CHECK THE SENDER DOMAIN — does it match exactly?
🔍 2. HOVER THE LINK — where does it actually go?
🔍 3. URGENCY + THREAT = RED FLAG — pressure is the weapon
🔍 4. GENERIC GREETING — 'Dear Customer' is a tell
🔍 5. MISMATCHED BRANDING — logos, fonts, colors slightly off
🔍 6. UNEXPECTED ATTACHMENT — especially .zip, .html, .docm
🔍 7. REPLY-TO DIFFERS FROM SENDER — classic spoofing signal

You don't need a security tool to catch most phishing attempts. You need 30 seconds and a suspicious mind.

Share this with someone who needs it today.

#PhishingAwareness #CyberHygiene #SecurityTraining #InfoSec #CyberEducation
results.hook: A forensic dissection of a real-looking phishing email transforms passive scrollers into active, empowered defenders.

results.caption:
When everything else fails — when the email slipped through, when the link was clicked, when the password was typed — one thing can still save you.

Phishing-Resistant MFA.

Not push notifications. Not SMS OTPs. Not TOTP codes.

Hardware security keys. FIDO2 passkeys. Certificate-based authentication.

These cannot be phished. The cryptographic binding to the legitimate domain means an attacker's fake site receives nothing — even if your user clicks every wrong thing.

🔐 FIDO2 / WebAuthn — W3C Standard
🔐 CISA Guidance: Phishing-Resistant MFA is mandatory for high-value targets
🔐 0 successful phishing attacks against FIDO2 hardware keys — ever.

The attack ends here.

Deploy FIDO2. Mandate hardware keys for privileged access. Make phishing cryptographically impossible.

This is the final image in our Phishing series. Which topic resonated most with your team?

#FIDO2 #PhishingResistant #MFA #ZeroTrust #CyberDefense
results.hook: A hardware security key glowing like a last beacon against darkness gives defenders a powerful, shareable symbol of protection.
